title: dactylorhiza incarnata var. straminea
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. straminea, also known as the autumn lady's tresses, is a rare and lesser-known variety of orchid native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata var. straminea include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the roots and leaves, and it can be used in teas, tinctures, and infusions.
